user_request: Please produce a progress-tracking HTML for the client
my_understanding:
I understand you want a skill that visualizes the counts of cc:WIP / cc:TODO / cc:done in Plans.md as a single HTML page.

The goal is an experience where a non-engineer client can grasp "how far along are we now" in 30 seconds.

options.name: Option A: grep Plans.md directly and render to HTML
options.summary: Aggregate simply with shell + jq. Fast to implement but limited extensibility
cons: gets complex if alert logic is bolted on later

options.name: Option B: add a new harness-progress skill via schema
options.summary: Structured via schema progress-snapshot.v1, integrated with render-html.sh
pros: easy to add alert / drift detect
cons: heavier initial implementation than Option A

risks.description: Risk of starting to edit files not listed in Plans.md
risks.mitigation: Detect via PostToolUse hook and show an alert

acceptance_criteria.description: The snapshot HTML shows the correct percentages for a fixture Plans.md (including each status)

confidence_evidence: 8 of the last 10 similar skill projects reached cc:done (80%)
DoD numeric-requirement coverage 90% (4.5 of 5 DoDs are machine-verifiable)
6 related D / P entries (D22, D37, P5, P8, P12, P29)

related_decisions.title: 3-layer memory architecture
related_decisions.relevance: Reference for a design that coexists with Layer 1 auto memory

similar_past_plans.relevance: Same 4-stage structure of skill skeleton + scripts + tests + e2e
